description: Advances: specled.package — cold verification of specled_-ci0 found the lint gate's corpus (skills/, docs/*.md, README.md) excludes .spec/: branch_guard_test_only_change (fabricated, same code specled_-ci0 removed from concepts.md) survives in .spec/specs/triangulation.spec.md:155 and .spec/decisions/specled.decision.finding_code_budget.md:47, and branch_guard_missing_subject_update_attested appears in a decision doc but is not emitted by lib. Extend test/docs_identifier_lint_test.exs corpus to .spec/**/*.md (decide policy for decision docs describing hypothetical/budgeted codes — maybe an explicit allow-marker), make docs glob recursive, and fix or annotate the three instances.

comments.text:
Impl complete (commit 4471e79). NOT closed.

What changed:
- test/docs_identifier_lint_test.exs: finding-code test corpus now = skills/**/*.md + docs/**/*.md (recursive) + README.md + .spec/**/*.md. Split corpus so the atom-severity test stays on user-facing docs only (skills/docs/README) — .spec scenarios quote atom-form config as fixtures under test, extending that check there would be false positives. Token patterns gained a (?<![\w/]) lookbehind so path segments aren't mistaken for codes.
- Reconciled fabricated codes (see per-code list below).
- New ADR .spec/decisions/specled.decision.doc_identifier_lint_spec_corpus.md; tightened requirement specled.package.doc_identifier_integrity in package.spec.md (required by branch_guard_missing_subject_update gate).

Allow-marker policy (DECIDED): a finding-code token that no detector emits is exempt ONLY when the same line carries an explicit, greppable, per-token marker `<!-- spec-lint:allow-code=<token> reason -->`. Exempts exactly that token on that one line — typos/removed codes still trip everywhere else. Chosen over a blanket .spec/ exemption (which defeats the ticket).

.spec/** codes the extended lint surfaced (4 distinct instances, not 3):
1. .spec/specs/triangulation.spec.md:155 `branch_guard_test_only_change` — FIXED (not annotated). It sat in an ACTIVE spec scenario `then:` as an expected outcome, but the covering integration test (test/integration/scenario_test_only_change_test.exs) asserts the opposite/real behavior: no branch_guard_untested_realization (closure still exercised) and no branch_guard_realization_drift. Rewrote the `then:` to the real codes. A spec contract shouldn't carry a lie, so fix > annotate here.
2. .spec/decisions/specled.decision.finding_code_budget.md:47 `branch_guard_test_only_change` — ANNOTATED (allow-marker). Legitimate budget doc listing an S1-planned code that was never emitted.
3. .spec/decisions/specled.decision.file_touch_yields_to_realization.md:108 `branch_guard_missing_subject_update_attested` — ANNOTATED (allow-marker). Rejected design alternative, never emitted.
4. .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md:25 `branch_guard_test` — FALSE POSITIVE, resolved by the lookbehind (not an annotation). It's the substring of the closure-file path test/specled_ex/config/branch_guard_test.exs, not a finding code. This one was NOT named in the ticket.
Plus: the new ADR itself names codes 1 and 3 as motivating examples → carries their allow-markers (dogfoods the convention).

Verification tier: change-scoped (final). Ran test/docs_identifier_lint_test.exs (2/2 pass) + full mix test (1029 tests, 0 failures — first full run surfaced my own ADR's unmarked example tokens, fixed, so finals_gt1) + mix spec.check --no-run-commands (status=pass, 0 findings) + mix format --check-formatted (clean) + mix deps.unlock --check-unused (clean) + mix compile --warnings-as-errors (clean). No verification stub flip needed — package spec's tagged_tests block already had execute: true covering doc_identifier_integrity.

Follow-ups: none required. Possible future hardening (not filed): the marker's reason text is unenforced (social); a lint could assert reason non-empty. Left as-is.

VERIFY: PASS

Session: cold-verify-6fn (worktree specled_ex-spec-doc-lint, opus-4.8)
Log: git diff main..4471e79 · Read package.spec.md + triangulation.spec.md + new ADR doc_identifier_lint_spec_corpus + docs_identifier_lint_test.exs + scenario_test_only_change_test.exs + branch_guard.spec.md · mix test test/docs_identifier_lint_test.exs (2/2 pass) · mix spec.check --no-run-commands (status=pass, findings=0) · scratch regex probe (7 cases) · grep .spec/** + lib/

Mechanical checks:
- Scope: 6 files, all in-scope (new policy ADR; finding_code_budget.md + file_touch_yields_to_realization.md allow-markers; package.spec.md must-tightening; triangulation.spec.md scenario rewrite; docs_identifier_lint_test.exs). Advances specled.package. No unrelated files. PASS
- mix test test/docs_identifier_lint_test.exs: 2 tests, 0 failures. PASS
- mix spec.check --no-run-commands: status=pass, errors=0, warnings=0, findings=0, change_type=single_subject, impacted=specled.package. PASS
- append_only detector clean (findings=0) => the must-tightening is not a prohibited weakening (mechanically confirmed). PASS

Semantic checks — Corpus-scoped claims: 6 validated, 4 by execution:
1. [by execution] Corpus extended: finding_code_corpus = guidance_files() ++ Path.wildcard(".spec/**/*.md"); docs glob now docs/**/*.md (recursive). Confirmed by reading + lint scanning .spec/** green.
2. [by execution] Allow-marker exempts EXACTLY the named token on that line; a marker for a different token does NOT rescue a fabricated prose code; genuine typos/removed codes still trip. Proven with a 7-case scratch probe replicating the exact @token_patterns/@allow_marker_pattern/unknown_tokens logic (in /tmp, tree untouched).
3. [by execution] Lookbehind (?<![\w/]) suppresses the path segment test/specled_ex/config/branch_guard_test.exs (branch_guard.spec.md:25, a surface-list closure path) without over-suppressing real backtick-delimited codes. Proven in probe.
4. triangulation.spec.md:155 rewrite reconciles a FABRICATED positive-outcome code (branch_guard_test_only_change, never emitted) to the two negative assertions its covering integration test actually makes — scenario_test_only_change_test.exs refutes branch_guard_untested_realization (subject) and branch_guard_realization_drift. New then:-clause matches verbatim. Both replacement codes are real (@known_codes). Legitimate reconciliation, not a laundered weakening.
5. [by execution] The two marked codes are genuinely unemitted by lib (grep for test_only_change / missing_subject_update_attested in lib/ returns nothing; the real base branch_guard_missing_subject_update IS emitted in branch_check.ex). Markers are load-bearing.
6. Reconciliation is corpus-complete (ceiling, not just the 4 named floor items): every remaining unemitted-token line in .spec/** carries its allow-marker, and the lint (which now scans the whole corpus) passes — so no unmarked fabricated code survives anywhere.

must coverage (specled.package.doc_identifier_integrity): covered by tagged_tests (execute:true) => the two @tag'd tests in docs_identifier_lint_test.exs. Failure modes: fabricated/typo/removed code in corpus -> test 1 fails; atom-form severity in user-facing YAML -> test 2 fails. Seam: @known_codes is deliberately hand-maintained (fail-loud, per ADR); allow-marker is greppable. Both green.

must-tightening (d): statement EXPANDED scope from 'Documentation and skill files' to also include .spec/** for finding-code integrity (genuine tightening); severity-form clause narrowed to 'user-facing docs' — not a weakening since the atom-severity corpus (skills/docs/README) is unchanged and .spec/** was never in it. severity_corpus = guidance_files() only; .spec fixtures correctly excluded. No contract weakened.

Verdict: PASS. Change is correctly scoped, the lint logic is sound and proven by execution, the scenario edit reconciles a fabricated code to its real covering-test assertions, and the must-tightening genuinely broadens integrity coverage without weakening any contract.

Notes: Follow-up noted by implementer (marker reason-text is socially enforced, not validated) is a reasonable non-blocking future hardening. Did not run full suite per instruction. Did not close/fix.

DEFERRED out of PR-2 to its own post-reset PR (user decision, account session-limit reached mid-fix). Cold verify PASS, BUT test-critic returned 2 BLOCKING findings: the allow-marker safety property is validated only by scanning the clean live corpus. REMAINING WORK (branch specled_-6fn/spec-doc-lint, worktree /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-spec-doc-lint, at 4471e79): (B1) controlled-input test that a fabricated UNMARKED code trips the lint; (B1b) a marked fabricated token is exempt; (B2) per-token scoping — marker for code_A must NOT rescue an unmarked fabricated code_B on the same line. Prefer testing extraction/exemption logic directly on controlled strings, not temp files; add a minimal @doc false seam if unknown_tokens/1 is private; do NOT weaken production check. THEN file a standalone follow-up for advisories A3 (lookbehind (?<![\w/]) over-suppression untested), A4 (severity-corpus .spec/** exclusion asymmetry untested), A5 (token char-class [a-z_] cannot represent digit-bearing codes). Then re-run test-critic, then own PR. NOT staged; left in_progress.

title: Extend doc-identifier lint to .spec/ and reconcile fabricated codes in spec corpus
